---
title: "Introduction"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
toc: true
execute:
  echo: false
---

```{r}
#| include: false
targets::tar_source('R')
```

Animal social dynamics have broad implications for survival, resource
availability and disease transmission. Methods and open source tools are
available for deriving social networks from animal telemetry data. These
approaches derive network structure from aggregated contact rates or home range
overlap. However, when used alone, these methods disregard further inferences on
intragroup social dynamics that can be derived from animal telemetry data.



Despite the number of developed metrics, increased computing power and
availability of animal telemetry data, there are few available tools in the R
language ecosystem for measuring intragroup dynamics. Here we present N (~11)
new functions for the R package {spatsoc} (Robitaille et al. 2019) for measuring
intragroup dynamics. We conducted a scoping review to identify metrics
applicable to spatiotemporal data used in ecological studies focused on dyadic
interactions, dominance hierarchies and leadership patterns (Sup A, B). Of the
resulting metrics, we selected those with a high number of citations (as a
measure of value for the target audience) and unavailability in the R
programming language, either through R packages or supplemental materials in the
literature. We will demonstrate these new functions through a case study and
discuss biological interpretation.
